zh.lifeSummary: Korber至少有1個兒子。他去世於1380年。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Korber至少有1个儿子。他去世于1380年。

nameMeanings.name: Korber
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Körber): occupational name for a basketmaker, from an agent derivative of Korb . Compare Koerber .
Slovenian: variant of   Korbar  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Kerper
meanings: German: habitational name for someone from any of the places called Kerpen in the Rhineland, Silesia, or former East Prussia.

variants.name: Korb
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from Middle High German korb, German Korb ‘basket’, applied as a metonymic occupational name for a basketmaker or for a peddler who carried his wares around in a basket, or as a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a basket. In Bavaria it also denoted the occupant of a laborer's cottage built with wattle (i.e. interwoven twigs) and daub.

variants.name: Kerber
meanings: German: variant of Körber (see Korber ) or Gerber .

variants.name: Korbar
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: occupational name for a basketmaker, derived from dialect korba ‘basket’, a word of German origin (see Korber ).

variants.name: Corber
meanings: English (Devon): perhaps an occupational name from an unrecorded Middle English word corber (an agent derivative of Old French courber ‘to bend something’), possibly denoting a basket maker.
Americanized form of German Korber or Körber.

variants.name: Koerber
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Lorenz, Otto, Theodor, Bernd, Erwin, Florian, Frieda, Inge, Ingeborg, Kurt.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (Körber): occupational name for a basket maker, from an agent derivative of Middle High German korp, German Korb ‘basket’. Compare Korber .
